prevalence.source: Coloproctology and primary care epidemiology syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable; higher with constipation and low fiber intake

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; substantial discomfort and productivity impact

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical evaluation with digital rectal exam and anoscopy; rule out other causes of bleeding

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; depends on access to outpatient procedures

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care diagnosis common; specialist proctology access limited outside urban centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Dietary fiber, hydration, topical therapy; rubber band ligation; hemorrhoidectomy for severe cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Stigma and delayed care; limited access to outpatient procedures
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Effective low-cost conservative treatments widely available
